Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Afroman Wins Civil Trial over Use of Police Raid Footage in His Music Videos 190 upvotes, 21 comments
  2. OpenRocket 660 upvotes, 113 comments
  3. Show HN: I built 48 lightweight SVG backgrounds you can copy/paste 335 upvotes, 63 comments
  4. Consensus Board Game 15 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. Autoresearch for SAT Solvers 149 upvotes, 29 comments
  6. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 318 upvotes, 78 comments
  7. Conway's Game of Life, in real life 246 upvotes, 63 comments
  8. Nvidia greenboost: transparently extend GPU VRAM using system RAM/NVMe 417 upvotes, 115 comments
  9. How many branches can your CPU predict? 49 upvotes, 9 comments
  10. Afroman found not liable in defamation case 604 upvotes, 219 comments
  11. Juggalo Makeup Blocks Facial Recognition Technology (2019) 80 upvotes, 24 comments
  12. Show HN: Duplicate 3 layers in a 24B LLM, logical deduction .22→.76. No training 186 upvotes, 70 comments
  13. Astral to Join OpenAI 386 upvotes, 175 comments
  14. Pretraining Language Models via Neural Cellular Automata 39 upvotes, 11 comments
  15. OpenBSD: PF queues break the 4 Gbps barrier 46 upvotes, 15 comments
  16. A sufficiently detailed spec is code 492 upvotes, 256 comments
  17. Eniac, the First General-Purpose Digital Computer, Turns 80 66 upvotes, 26 comments
  18. OpenAI to Acquire Astral 135 upvotes, 68 comments
  19. 'Your Frustration Is the Product' 157 upvotes, 83 comments
  20. LotusNotes 137 upvotes, 71 comments
  21. The next fight over the use of facial recognition could be in the supermarkets 23 upvotes, 7 comments
  22. Warranty Void If Regenerated 446 upvotes, 270 comments
  23. The math that explains why bell curves are everywhere 174 upvotes, 103 comments
  24. Nvidia NemoClaw 348 upvotes, 229 comments
  25. Stdwin: Standard window interface by Guido Van Rossum [pdf] 59 upvotes, 33 comments
  26. Gluon: Explicit Performance 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. WFH is becoming a benefit again 26 upvotes, 14 comments
  28. 2% of ICML papers desk rejected because the authors used LLM in their reviews 142 upvotes, 130 comments
  29. Austin’s surge of new housing construction drove down rents 658 upvotes, 791 comments
  30. Iran war energy shock sparks global push to reduce fossil fuel dependence 187 upvotes, 224 comments

Last generated 11:00pm, 19 March 2026. Source here.
